source.title: Association of the MUC5B Promoter Polymorphism With Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis

description: MUC5B rs35705950 is the strongest genetic risk factor for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The minor T allele increases MUC5B expression in terminal bronchi and is associated with substantially elevated IPF risk.

GG.recommendation: Standard pulmonary health; avoid environmental lung toxins; smoking cessation if applicable

GT.effect: Heterozygous carrier; ~6.8-9.0x increased risk for familial/sporadic IPF
GT.recommendation: Baseline high-resolution CT chest; annual pulmonary function testing; occupational exposure assessment; smoking cessation strongly recommended; family counseling

TG.effect: Heterozygous carrier; ~6.8-9.0x increased risk for familial/sporadic IPF
TG.recommendation: Baseline high-resolution CT chest; annual pulmonary function testing; occupational exposure assessment; smoking cessation strongly recommended; family counseling

TT.effect: Homozygous for risk allele; ~20.8-21.8x increased risk; dramatically elevated disease susceptibility
TT.recommendation: Baseline HRCT and PFTs immediately; referral to IPF specialist; antifibrotic therapy consideration (pirfenidone or nintedanib) even for subclinical disease; close monitoring every 3-6 months; strict avoidance of environmental triggers; genetic counseling for family members
